chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA Constt: Petition No. S-61 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 01. For orders on office objection "A". 02. For orders on M.A.No.166/2026 (E/A). 03. For hearing of main case. 06.03.2026 Mr. Muhammad Afzal Jagirani, Advocate for the petitioner. *=.*=.*=.*=.*=.*=.*=.*=* Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the official respondents are allegedly harassing the petitioner. He submits that in this regard the petitioner had approached the learned Additional Sessions Judge-II/Ex-officio Justice of Peace, Kandhkot, who vide order dated 08.01.2026 directed the concerned officials to provide legal protection to the petitioner. Learned counsel, however, contends that despite such order the petitioner apprehends harassment at the hands of the official respondents, therefore the present petition has been filed. Heard learned counsel for the petitioner and perused the material placed on record. It appears from the record that the petitioner has already availed the remedy before the learned Ex-officio Justice of Peace, who has passed an order directing the concerned officials to provide legal protection to the petitioner. It is well settled that the constitutional jurisdiction of this Court is discretionary in nature and is ordinarily not exercised where an adequate and efficacious remedy has already been availed and appropriate directions have been issued by the competent forum. Since the petitioner has already been granted the relief sought through the order dated 08.01.2026, no further direction is called for in the present proceedings. Consequently, the instant petition being not maintainable is dismissed in limine, along with the listed applications.

Since the petitioner has already been granted the relief sought through the order dated 08.01.2026, no further direction is called for in the present proceedings. Consequently, the instant petition being not maintainable is dismissed in limine, along with the listed applications. Needless to observe that in case of any violation of the order dated 08.01.2026, the petitioner shall be at liberty to seek appropriate remedy in accordance with law. JUDGE
